125521.explanation: We choose the best people based on quality and commitment, not on gender. Diversity is important, but coercion does not suit us.

125522.explanation: We are rooted in society and listen to our residents. The voice of Veldhoven citizens must be heard in important decisions.

125525.explanation: We allow small-scale businesses and think in terms of possibilities. Farmers must be able to operate their businesses with respect for the environment.

125529.explanation: Employers who attract workers also have a responsibility for their housing. This alleviates pressure on the housing market.

125530.explanation: We want to better protect trees with a clear plan. The municipality sets a good example by preventing unnecessary tree felling.

125531.explanation: We trust in the personal responsibility of residents. Banning is not part of our pragmatic approach.

125535.explanation: Events promote liveliness and can count on our support. We facilitate organizers rather than restrict them.

125537.explanation: We build a lot, but in the right places. Dispersed housing on agricultural land does not fit with preserving our green character.

125540.explanation: Good education deserves good facilities. School buildings must be well maintained and also serve as meeting places.

125544.explanation: Safety is important, but more authorized officers (BOAs) is not automatically the solution. We focus on neighborhood watch and involvement.

125545.explanation: An attentive neighborhood is a safe neighborhood. We trust in neighborhood watch and active involvement instead of extra cameras.

125547.explanation: Young people from Veldhoven should be able to continue living here. Local ties are important and families should be able to stay together.

125549.explanation: The housing shortage is enormous. If people want to live in holiday homes without causing nuisance, we should allow it. Pragmatism above bureaucracy.

125550.explanation: We need to build a lot, but in the right locations. This requires tailored solutions per situation, while preserving our green character.

125553.explanation: We offer tailored support to vulnerable residents, but raising limits means higher costs for all residents. Balance is important.

125555.explanation: Recently new locations have been designated and developed to prepare Veldhoven for the future. At this moment we see no reason to designate more parking spaces.

125556.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount, but a 30 km/h zone everywhere is too rigid. We choose tailored solutions per neighborhood and situation.

125557.explanation: Appropriate care is important, but always guaranteeing full freedom of choice is not realistic. Quality of care comes first.

125560.explanation: We keep local taxes as low as possible. Dog facilities are paid for collectively, just like other amenities, without separate charges for one group.

125563.explanation: A lively Veldhoven requires flexibility. Entrepreneurs must be able to decide themselves when they want to open.

125564.explanation: We fulfill our social responsibility and are bound by national legislation in doing so. This is not a choice for the municipality.

125566.explanation: We invest in sustainability. Solar panels on agricultural land can, under conditions, contribute to the energy transition without harming the greenery.

125567.explanation: The library is valuable and has been recently renewed. We maintain what we have and look for smart solutions.

125569.explanation: Swimming is important, but this is primarily the responsibility of parents. As a safety net, Stichting Leergeld can be called upon if needed.

125571.explanation: This does not suit the character of Veldhoven as a family-friendly municipality. We see no added value for our residents.

125575.explanation: In every neighborhood we want a meeting place where people can see, talk to, and help each other. School buildings, sports facilities, and community centers can be used for this purpose.

125576.explanation: We want to retain two hours of free parking. An accessible center is important for residents and entrepreneurs.

125577.explanation: There are currently enough spaces. During transformations, extra spaces will be added where necessary, but the focus is on encouraging pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport.

125579.explanation: We listen to residents and seek tailored solutions, but the housing shortage is urgent. Good involvement in advance, decisiveness when needed.

125580.explanation: Cyclists and pedestrians receive priority in neighborhoods. We are expanding bike parking and ensuring safe routes.

125521.explanation: For GBV, the quality of the person is paramount. We look at the suitability of the candidate alderman regardless of gender. A balanced distribution is a nice goal but should not be a mandatory requirement.

125522.explanation: GBV wants to actively involve residents, but not through referenda. Complex plans, especially regarding the design of the Veldhoven living environment, are unsuitable for a simple 'yes' or 'no'. We choose forms of participation that allow more nuance and dialogue.

125525.explanation: GBV is positive about entrepreneurship and side activities on farms if these fit within the surroundings. We want to give entrepreneurs space, but the word 'always' goes too far for us. Each situation will be looked at individually.

125529.explanation: Housing seasonal workers in agriculture and horticulture on the company’s own premises is a good idea. The condition is that the housing is safe and of good quality.

125530.explanation: GBV cherishes Veldhoven's greenery and favors a balance in which trees are preserved as much as possible. If a tree is cut down, GBV believes it should be compensated.

125531.explanation: GBV does not want a ban on wood stoves. However, the municipality can more actively promote tools like the 'Stookwijzer' in the interest of good air quality to prevent nuisance.

125535.explanation: Veldhoven already has rules regarding noise at events, such as the fair. Stricter rules are not necessary for GBV; however, adequate enforcement of those rules is important.

125537.explanation: GBV only supports this if the environmental quality improves. For example, by demolishing old stables or creating new nature.

125540.explanation: GBV believes every child has the right to a good school building close to home. Renovating all schools within four years is financially unfeasible. Moreover, some schools do not require it as their buildings are relatively new.

125544.explanation: To effectively tackle nuisance in the residential neighborhoods of Veldhoven, GBV wants to invest more in enforcement officers (boa’s). This contributes to a more livable neighborhood.

125545.explanation: GBV believes prevention is better than cure, but talking alone does not solve nuisance problems. That is why GBV wants to invest in more enforcement officers and street camera surveillance to directly address nuisance issues.

125547.explanation: Young people in Veldhoven deserve a fair chance to find housing in our municipality. GBV wants research into how we can concretely increase opportunities for our first-time buyers, including seriously considering priority for local residents.

125549.explanation: There are national developments regarding permanent residence in recreational parks. GBV wants to first wait for the implementation from The Hague. After that, we will determine what is desirable and feasible for Veldhoven.

125550.explanation: GBV wants more housing quickly, but with preservation of the village character in the four church villages. Therefore, we build within the built-up area: for example, along the urban axis, in the City Center, and at the Run.

125553.explanation: GBV prefers to focus on reaching residents who already qualify for income support but do not make use of these schemes. This happens too often. We focus on better information and early detection to prevent debts, rather than just raising the income threshold.

125555.explanation: The municipality should assess what each neighborhood needs. Currently, charging stations are sometimes installed where they are seldom used. GBV wants tailor-made solutions. We want Veldhoven residents to be able to charge their electric cars with their own electricity, for example using cable duct tiles.

125556.explanation: GBV opposes a general reduction to 30 km/h. Therefore, GBV maintains 70 km/h on the Kempenbaan and Heerbaan, and 50 km/h on connecting roads with separated bike paths such as the Sondervick, Provincialeweg, Dorpstraat, Sterrenlaan, and Burgemeester van Hoofflaan.

125557.explanation: GBV believes residents should have as much freedom of choice as possible within the available options.

125560.explanation: The dog tax has been significantly reduced (2024: €95.94 → 2025: €50.00 per dog) and is used for, among other things, dog walking areas and enforcement. GBV believes these are basic facilities and therefore wants to abolish the dog tax.

125563.explanation: In principle, GBV supports more shopping Sundays. However, we believe this must be determined in good consultation with entrepreneurs. Support from local retailers is important to us.

125564.explanation: In principle, GBV wants to abolish the priority scheme. However, this is only feasible if the assignment from the national government is less strict. A relaxation from The Hague is therefore necessary.

125566.explanation: GBV follows the solar ladder. This means: first solar panels on roofs, grounds, and objects. Only if that is insufficient does agricultural land come into consideration. We do not want to prohibit this upfront but want to keep it as a last option.

125567.explanation: The current accommodation of the library is due for replacement or renovation. GBV believes the library deserves a place in a new cultural hotspot to be developed in the center, which has been widely discussed in recent years.

125569.explanation: GBV wants to allocate money for school swimming lessons, provided the schools are willing to organize this again.

125571.explanation: GBV sees no necessity for establishing a coffee shop in Veldhoven.

125575.explanation: GBV believes there is room in Veldhoven neighborhoods for recognizable meeting places and future-proof community centers, fitting the neighborhood’s needs.

125576.explanation: GBV opposes making parking more expensive in the city center. With the blue zone and the first 2 hours free parking, the City Center remains accessible and attractive for visitors and entrepreneurs.

125577.explanation: More homes simply mean more cars. GBV is realistic: the car remains important. With 1,000 new homes, parking capacity must grow proportionally to prevent parking chaos.

125579.explanation: Residents must be involved as early as possible in building plans. The municipality must ensure this really happens. However, no false expectations should arise. Participating in thinking is not the same as co-deciding. GBV wants faster construction of more homes in Veldhoven.

125580.explanation: The municipality must encourage walking and cycling more. This starts with safe sidewalks and bike paths. In some places, good footpaths are missing or bike paths are poor. Therefore, GBV wants extra funds for maintenance and safe routes.

125521.explanation: Quality and experience are more important.

125522.explanation: The municipal council is democratically elected to make decisions.

125525.explanation: According to the Veldhoven VVD, entrepreneurship must be given space.

125529.explanation: This is not really an issue in Veldhoven.

125530.explanation: In public areas, this should happen where possible, but we certainly do not want to make this mandatory on private property.

125531.explanation: The Veldhoven VVD supports freedom of choice, provided there is no nuisance.

125535.explanation: Events bring life and conviviality to Veldhoven. They foster encounters, strengthen our associations, and also provide economic benefits for horeca and shops.

125537.explanation: The Veldhoven VVD believes space usage should be flexible.

125540.explanation: Good education deserves good accommodation. This is already planned.

125544.explanation: We find it important that police and special enforcement officers (BOA’s) are visible on the streets. The supervision and enforcement department monitors compliance with rules. This can concern illegally parked cars or youth causing nuisance.

125545.explanation: Only if it demonstrably contributes to safety and prevention.

125547.explanation: We believe residents of our municipality should have priority on new homes. Many people living here cannot find affordable housing. They are forced to leave Veldhoven, while their work, family, and friends are here.

125549.explanation: Unfortunately, Veldhoven also faces a major housing shortage. This is visible among others by the many people who have to live in holiday parks. Allowing permanent residence in holiday parks can help partially solve the housing shortage at this time.

125550.explanation: This is necessary, where possible, to tackle the tight housing market.

125553.explanation: We want to help people in poverty in a targeted way, but not with generic policy.

125556.explanation: The Veldhoven VVD wants safe streets, especially in residential areas. A 30 km/h limit is often appropriate there. But on main roads within Veldhoven, the VVD wants to maintain 50 km/h or 70 km/h. This keeps traffic flowing and Veldhoven accessible. So not 30 everywhere, but tailor-made per street.

125560.explanation: Dog tax is an unfair tax, since you do not pay tax for, for example, a cat. For this reason, many municipalities have already abolished the dog tax.

125563.explanation: Freedom of choice for entrepreneurs and consumers. There can also be a more flexible approach to shopping hours on Sundays.

125564.explanation: Disagree. As far as we are concerned, Veldhoven residents themselves should be given priority.

125566.explanation: First cover the roofs of homes and business buildings. Eventually, homes can be built on agricultural land.

125567.explanation: The library should become a cultural hotspot, among other things as a meeting place and knowledge center.

125569.explanation: The safety of our children is paramount.

125575.explanation: Not necessary or feasible everywhere. We must first ensure that existing village centers and community centers can continue to exist.

125576.explanation: The Veldhoven VVD places great value on good accessibility of the City Center for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors alike. Free parking in the center should therefore remain free in our view.

125577.explanation: The Veldhoven VVD places great value on good accessibility of the City Center for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors alike. Fewer parking spaces can lead to fewer visitors and harm the local economy.

125579.explanation: Residents must be able to have a say in housing construction. The municipality of Veldhoven must listen to residents. But we need new houses quickly, so it should not take too much additional time to avoid delays in building plans. Currently, we already have an extensive participation process.

125521.explanation: The primary focus is on quality. This can result in more women than men in the council. But also more men than women. With equal assessment, a woman would be preferred.

125522.explanation: A referendum lacks nuance. There is no dialogue. While administrative decisions require nuance and conversation. To arrive at a solution together. That is why there are many good alternative instruments that enable dialogue and empower residents.

125525.explanation: The rural area must be cherished. More concrete and commerce cannot be the default attitude. This must always be subject to broader visions, natural values, and environmental impacts. A broad interest approach is a prerequisite.

125529.explanation: Housing laborers is a social responsibility. Especially since it is difficult to find laborers. This means many agriculture and horticulture businesses rely on external solutions and seasonal workers. Together we share the responsibility to arrange proper housing.

125530.explanation: As far as GroenLinks & PvdA Veldhoven are concerned, we only cut down trees if necessary. For every tree cut down, we plant two trees in return. This preserves greenery in public spaces and rural areas, which is important for biodiversity, good health, and clean air.

125531.explanation: The use of wood stoves is bad for the environment and does not contribute to clean air. We have a responsibility to make homes more sustainable and reduce emissions. Wood burning does not fit here, unless it meets sustainability and clean air criteria.

125535.explanation: Festivals and events contribute to connection and gathering. They therefore have great social value. Noise standards are sufficiently strict and defined. At the same time, the frequency of nuisance should be prevented from increasing, for example with innovative solutions.

125537.explanation: The rural area must be cherished. More concrete cannot be the default attitude. This must always be subject to broader visions, natural values, the housing task, and environmental impacts. A broad interest approach is a prerequisite.

125540.explanation: GroenLinks & PvdA Veldhoven believes all children should be able to grow up with opportunities. Good and accessible education plays an important role, as does its housing. Therefore, in our view, we will invest substantially in the condition and accessibility of education in the coming years.

125544.explanation: Currently, there are enough enforcement officers. We prefer to focus more on a pleasant and safe neighborhood through prevention and interventions in public space, such as lighting, neighborhood prevention networks, and neighborhood work. These have been proven more effective in preventing insecurity.

125545.explanation: For safe neighborhoods, we want to invest in interventions in public spaces. Camera surveillance is an option, as are lighting and prevention networks. In light of social insecurity for women, together with the VVD, we have already raised attention to this.

125547.explanation: At present, 50% of homes can be allocated to local residents. Research shows that in Veldhoven this percentage is already higher. Therefore, it is not logical to impose a minimum now, given the legal possibilities are lower than the current allocation to local residents.

125549.explanation: GroenLinks & PvdA Veldhoven believes that living in holiday parks, given the great housing shortage, should be temporarily allowed, with a phase-out arrangement.

125550.explanation: We believe building should occur within the built-up area, sparing the green rural area as much as possible. In practice, there is almost no space left here. New construction must be realized on vacant spots, former office buildings, or other available locations.

125553.explanation: We believe we must support residents in poverty well, so they can overcome financial problems and fully participate in society. For this reason, various social benefit schemes have been increased, and at our initiative, research was done into increasing special assistance.

125555.explanation: GroenLinks & PvdA Veldhoven believes that to stimulate electric driving, providing sufficient charging points is essential. Therefore, we place enough charging stations in public spaces and make private use easier where necessary.

125556.explanation: A lower speed contributes to traffic safety and a healthier living environment. However, making all built-up areas 30 km/h is not our preference. We do want this in all neighborhoods, with main roads such as the Heerbaan remaining at the standard 50 km/h.

125557.explanation: As long as the resident themselves finances it, they should also be able to decide which care provider suits them best. When the municipality finances it, customization cannot always be provided. That would make care and thus social costs too expensive.

125560.explanation: Nationally, the trend is that municipalities abolish the dog tax because it leads to a disproportionate distribution of burdens and financial barriers for dog owners. At the same time, the municipality does incur costs exclusively for dog owners. If abolished, these costs must be critically evaluated.

125563.explanation: Shopkeepers should have the freedom to decide when they are open. If an extra shopping Sunday can contribute to a better business model and also gives residents more flexibility, we should certainly be open to that.

125564.explanation: As far as GroenLinks-PvdA Veldhoven is concerned, we determine priority based on urgency, as we currently do. Status holders can also qualify. Refugees usually come from often temporary and busy stays in the Netherlands with poorer conditions and must move on. They are eligible for priority in such cases. Other groups, such as people in conflict separation or domestic violence, sometimes urgently seek housing. Clear criteria apply, which we will continue to uphold. These apply to all target groups that qualify.

125566.explanation: We believe placing solar panels on agricultural land should be allowed. There is already a solar meadow in Veldhoven supplying a lot of energy to society. Given shortages, these kinds of solutions are very important and therefore also possible.

125567.explanation: Partly at our urging, a Library Vision has been delivered recently. This aims towards the library as a knowledge hub, with education, meeting, and relaxation. The library is already well on its way and will continue to focus on this in coming years.

125569.explanation: School swimming is a primary life skill and a broad social responsibility. Money should not be a barrier to offering swimming lessons at school. We specifically support children and families for whom this is currently a barrier.

125571.explanation: A coffeeshop is less suited to the size and dynamic of Veldhoven. At the same time, there is responsibility to organize this properly. Regional policy, with regional agreements, could help, ensuring the rights of Veldhoven residents are respected.

125575.explanation: We want an accessible community center in every neighborhood as the social heart of the neighborhood. As a center for meeting and support, matching residents' needs. Each community center will have a neighborhood coordinator as a point of contact, who works with residents to build a connected neighborhood.

125576.explanation: Currently, parking is free for the first 2 hours. This will remain for now. It is legitimate to reconsider in a few years whether all residents should contribute to maintenance and parking costs as they currently do, while encouraging walking, cycling, and public transport.

125577.explanation: The redevelopment of the City Center does not require more parking spaces than necessary, and the current supply suffices. We choose to add green spaces and housing, for an attractive center to live and recreate. We want to encourage walking, cycling, and public transport.

125579.explanation: We believe residents should be well involved from the start in new policies and genuinely be able to think along, discuss, and decide. The level of participation may vary per project, but we always want to involve residents well. We want to avoid lengthy delaying procedures.

125580.explanation: We are in favor of promoting walking and cycling. It contributes to accessibility, and is healthier and safer for residents and the climate. We do this by prioritizing walking, cycling, and public transport. Daily amenities are reachable within 15 minutes.

125521.explanation: But knowledge takes precedence over gender.

125522.explanation: Residents can elect their representatives once every 4 years.

125525.explanation: This depends heavily on the local situation.

125529.explanation: But then in a humane manner.

125530.explanation: There is already legislation for this.

125535.explanation: Noise is allowed but must meet the requirements.

125537.explanation: There are legal rules for this.

125545.explanation: Only in those areas where it is truly necessary.

125547.explanation: Because, for example, they can then become informal caregivers, and knowing each other makes it safer and increases social cohesion.

125549.explanation: Especially in this time of housing shortage.

125550.explanation: Building within the existing village boundaries causes all small pieces of necessary green space to be lost to concrete.

125555.explanation: Where do we get all that electricity from?

125560.explanation: Not possible due to costs, among others for dog walking areas.

125563.explanation: But the initiative remains with the shopkeepers themselves.

125564.explanation: We will not do anything extra for these people.

125566.explanation: It is a form of sustainable electricity generation, but we prefer to generate this elsewhere/in another way (for example on roofs).

125567.explanation: A new plan has already been drafted for this.

125569.explanation: A healthy contribution for this is responsible.

125571.explanation: There are plenty within 15 minutes' distance from Veldhoven.

125575.explanation: This is partly for the necessary social contacts.

125576.explanation: This is bad for the shopkeepers.

125577.explanation: There is currently sufficient parking space.

125579.explanation: The Municipality is there for the residents.

125580.explanation: With good walking and cycling paths, we already sufficiently promote this.

125521.explanation: We find diversity important, especially in municipal government. For us, diversity is more than just gender ratios. We also consider aspects like age, education, and work experience.

125522.explanation: We highly value input and participation from residents. A referendum is difficult: the question must lead to a yes/no answer, nuances, reasoning, and 'no, but' options are lost. That is why we opt for strengthened coordination of participation, with experiments like a residents’ panel.

125525.explanation: We believe the municipality has a role in the transition of the rural area to a vital green living environment. This also means supporting farmers in the step towards other forms of entrepreneurship. We must look per location in Veldhoven what is possible; not everything will be possible everywhere.

125529.explanation: We believe seasonal workers deserve good accommodation. Employers who employ or bring these seasonal workers to Veldhoven must also provide good housing. This can also happen on their own company premises.

125530.explanation: Trees are important for Veldhoven. Felling often has a good reason, but we want to remain green. Each year, there must be a net increase in trees planted compared to those we cut down. We plant them not only in rural areas but especially in built-up areas to combat heat stress and for a more beautiful living environment.

125531.explanation: We want to better inform residents about burning behaviour, among other things via the Burning Guide. Before considering a ban, we prefer to first properly inform people and then address their behaviour when necessary.

125535.explanation: We want to continue investing in events and activities in Veldhoven. That promotes meetings, social contacts, and integration of new residents. The rules for this must be clear and consistently enforced. We see no reason for stricter or more lenient rules.

125537.explanation: We must support the transition to a vital, green rural area. Farmers who stop or downsize can build a few homes as compensation, provided this strengthens the overall living environment. Custom solutions remain necessary: not every location is suitable.

125540.explanation: We stand for pleasant, quality school buildings for every child and every teacher in Veldhoven. Because many locations are outdated, we gladly work on new construction and thorough renovations or expansions. A good learning environment is truly important to us.

125544.explanation: BOAs are indispensable for making Veldhoven a pleasant place to live. The current capacity is insufficient to be visibly present in Veldhoven for safety and to fulfill all other tasks, such as combating, for example, illegal waste dumping.

125545.explanation: We believe that camera surveillance is a significant measure. There are places where it can genuinely contribute to reducing insecurity and nuisance. However, in our view, this approach is not the ultimate solution for structural problems.

125547.explanation: Priority for Veldhoven residents sounds good, but it can backfire for many starters who studied in the city. We want to maintain regional freedom so that everyone can live flexibly. The real solution to the housing shortage is not exclusion, but simply: build, build, build!

125549.explanation: Permanent residence in holiday parks is undesirable, but due to the housing shortage, we will not evict certain current residents. They will receive personal security against enforcement. This does not apply to new cases; the only structural solution is truly building more new homes.

125550.explanation: In Veldhoven, we see the City Center line, Burgemeester van Hoofflaan, and De Run as the places where by far most new homes should be built. In terms of mobility and facilities, this is simply the best place to do this well.

125553.explanation: We believe that people who really need it should be able to ask the municipality for help with their poverty problems. The municipality currently uses the 130% social assistance norm as the income limit. Much help is also available to every resident through our social partners, regardless of income.

125555.explanation: Electric driving is the future, but charging in Veldhoven is difficult without a private driveway. To stimulate the switch to electric driving for residents and visitors, more public charging stations are needed. We will maintain a balance: sufficient parking space must remain available for all neighborhood residents.

125556.explanation: We want the car to be a guest: a lower speed makes our neighborhoods safer and more pleasant to live in; that should be the standard here. But on main through roads (for example, Kempenbaan and Heerbaan), we see it differently. These roads gather all traffic from Veldhoven and must handle all that traffic efficiently within limited space.

125557.explanation: The demand for care is growing rapidly, so additional requirements for care providers are not realistic. However, it is crucial that they have knowledge of and understanding of diverse backgrounds and beliefs whenever this affects care. This way, care in Veldhoven remains accessible and respectful for everyone.

125560.explanation: We support maintaining the dog tax so that it at least partly covers the costs of maintenance and cleaning of dog walking fields in Veldhoven.

125563.explanation: We believe that entrepreneurs in Veldhoven should decide which days they want to serve customers. We see no reason to limit the number of shopping Sundays.

125564.explanation: We have a legal task to provide housing for a certain number of status holders in Veldhoven each year. We do not avoid this and understand the frustration it causes some. If the legislator requires giving priority, we will do so; if the legislator wants to organize it differently, we will execute accordingly. We believe the national government is in the lead, and we await clarification. Building homes at an ambitious pace is the real solution in our view.

125566.explanation: We choose solar-on-roof in Veldhoven; there is still great potential here, even if roofs need reinforcement first. We are not in favor of solar panels on agricultural land: that scarce space is too valuable to be used solely for energy generation.

125567.explanation: A few years ago, the idea arose to build a cultural hotspot combining the library, De Schalm, and Museum ’t Oude Slot. We fully support this. This hotspot would fit beautifully in the renewed Meiveld.

125569.explanation: Swimming is an essential basic skill in our water-rich country. Therefore, we want to bring back school swimming in Veldhoven. This is especially important for children of internationals, for whom swimming lessons are less self-evident.

125571.explanation: Currently, Veldhoven does not allow coffeeshops. If an entrepreneur wants to open one, we believe the discussion should focus on where and under what conditions, not on a permanent ban. However, due to the tolerance policy and resulting illegal cultivation, we cannot locally be clear supporters at this time.

125575.explanation: Due to aging and growing care demand, we want to strengthen self-reliance in Veldhoven. Neighborhood centers are crucial as meeting points, organizational hubs, and nearby contact places between residents, institutions, and the municipality- for young and old alike. Not every neighborhood needs one, but they should be within short distance.

125576.explanation: Free parking does not exist; it costs the community hundreds of thousands in the City Center. We want to encourage cycling and public transport and discourage car use. A parking fee works as a small incentive to cycle more often, which is better for accessibility and livability in Veldhoven.

125577.explanation: We truly favor cycling and public transport. Cars remain welcome but less dominant. In a greener City Center, we prefer more trees over many more parking spaces. Space for cars is no longer a given; we prioritize a healthy, green living environment for all residents.

125579.explanation: We highly value participation in construction projects. A good process where residents can share ideas and concerns is crucial-often resulting in better plans. We want the municipality to take control of the participation process instead of the developers.

125580.explanation: We believe walking and cycling should be the standard in Veldhoven. We want to invest in good, well-lit paths, enough benches, and safe bicycle parking. By providing the right infrastructure, we make healthy and sustainable movement attractive and safe for every resident.

125521.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven chooses quality over mandatory balance. We find a good representation of the population important for both council and college. Diversity is a broader issue than just the distribution of men and women.

125522.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven opts for different forms of participation. For example, we support citizens' assemblies on specific topics, so residents can contribute ideas on a matter. We are not in favor of referendums since those test policy only after it has been made.

125525.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes that in the current situation, it is difficult for farmers in many ways. Income from other activities is a good idea.

125529.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes it is good if employers take responsibility for housing. Mandatory shelter on their own premises is not always the best solution.

125530.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven is concerned that a tree-for-a-tree rule will be an excuse to cut down valuable trees. Planting one tree elsewhere then suffices for felling. However, a young tree takes a long time to contribute to CO2 capture. We prefer other green measures.

125531.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes people should not suffer nuisance from stoves. We find a complete ban too drastic a measure.

125535.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven chooses to maintain current standards. Parties and festivals are important: they connect people and are vital for hospitality.

125537.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes we must be careful with our rural area. Whether housing is possible depends on the specific situation.

125540.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes Veldhoven has good buildings. We should consider growth in supply in the coming period, possibly expanding rather than just improving.

125544.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes the number of BOAs should grow alongside the municipality's growth.

125545.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven thinks more cameras do not automatically lead to more safety. You also need people to respond to the footage. Additional camera surveillance can be useful in specific situations.

125547.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes priority should be given to urgent cases. Prioritizing local residents can cause waiting times to increase because neighboring municipalities might do the same.

125549.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes holiday homes are not intended as permanent residences.

125550.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes we must be careful with the rural area.

125553.explanation: In Veldhoven, this limit is already higher than the legal limit. CDA Local Veldhoven sees this positively and wants to keep it that way.

125555.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes that the current agreements within Veldhoven are sufficient.

125556.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven supports 30 km/h speed limits in built-up areas. It provides more safety.

125557.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes that we must not discriminate against each other. People can bridge cultural differences well together without needing specific rules for it.

125560.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven supports more modern forms of taxation. The dog tax dates from another era. The municipality should seek alternatives.

125563.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven wants to maintain the current number of shopping Sundays.

125564.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes this measure should fall within the existing national agreements. People with a residence permit should be able to participate as soon as possible.

125566.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven thinks that solar panels on farmland can contribute to the energy transition. It also provides farmers with an alternative source of income.

125567.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven is proud of the library in Veldhoven. The library plays an important role and deserves support.

125569.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes that school swimming means a lot of money is spent on swimming lessons for children who already know how to swim. However, we want to emphasize the importance of swimming lessons and look at how they can be accessible to all children.

125571.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven thinks a coffeeshop sends the wrong signal. We support a healthy environment and a smoke-free generation.

125575.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven thinks it is important that there are sufficient meeting places. These locations provide associations and clubs with space to organize activities.

125576.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven sees paid parking and the parking fee level as a sub-question within the plans for the redesign of the City Center. The answer depends on the final plans.

125577.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven considers it more important that sufficient housing, greenery, and bicycle parking are included in the redesign.

125579.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes that good participation is important in new construction projects.

125580.explanation: CDA Local Veldhoven believes that a healthy and clean environment with good walking and cycling paths encourages more physical activity and less frequent car use for short distances.

125521.explanation: It would be great if that succeeds, but of course that also depends on the number of women who become candidates.

125522.explanation: This is a national SP position. A referendum is a good instrument to let the residents of Veldhoven express their opinion.

125525.explanation: These are additional incomes for a farmer, and if that also reduces the livestock, then that is an added bonus.

125529.explanation: This leads to exploitation. If they lose their job, they also become homeless immediately.

125530.explanation: Our trees are the lungs of the earth, and we must take good care of them. So when trees are cut down, the same number must be replanted.

125531.explanation: For people living in energy poverty, this is a good solution. However, we must consider the emissions from this, so they should be minimized as much as possible.

125535.explanation: We should tolerate those few days per year when cultural activities take place. These activities promote connection.

125537.explanation: But only under strict conditions, such as affordable housing for everyone, not just for family.

125540.explanation: If these (schools) are not yet up to standard regarding insulation and air quality, action must be taken quickly. This concerns our youth and teachers. They are the future and must be able to grow up healthily.

125544.explanation: Unfortunately, this is necessary; there are too many minor offenses that cannot be addressed due to a shortage of enforcement officers (BOAs).

125545.explanation: If there is not enough money to appoint enough BOAs, then camera surveillance is a good (temporary) measure. Cameras also provide a feeling of safety.

125547.explanation: Too many young people cannot start their future. But status holders must also have a chance at housing.

125549.explanation: There is a housing crisis, so we must use all available space to accommodate housing.

125550.explanation: Due to the housing crisis and the steadily growing population, partly because of knowledge workers, we must use as much space as possible to build. However, the green space that disappears must be fully compensated.

125553.explanation: Middle incomes are also struggling. Prices of groceries and energy keep rising.

125555.explanation: These charging spots are often empty once the car is charged. There are many parking problems and many people cannot afford an electric car.

125556.explanation: But better research into traffic safety, noise nuisance, and the environment must come first.

125557.explanation: Many fellow citizens have different religious beliefs and therefore different customs; we must take this into account.

125560.explanation: This is the balancing item in the municipality’s budget. It is better to investigate how the budget can actually be balanced.

125563.explanation: There is more than enough opportunity to shop. There is a shortage of staff, and shopkeepers also need enough time to rest from a workweek.

125564.explanation: These people cannot live in shelters forever. The percentage that gets housing is less than 10%. So there should be enough homes left for our youth and other housing seekers. That would be the ideal situation; unfortunately, there is a housing crisis and too few homes.

125566.explanation: We must generate as much electricity as possible, but this can only happen once the power grid has been sufficiently expanded.

125567.explanation: The library has undergone several renovations and is currently sufficiently equipped for the future. Only if major necessary changes are needed will the library have to be renewed. This must be thoroughly researched to determine whether the costs outweigh the benefits.

125569.explanation: There are too many children who cannot swim. Not all parents are aware of the dangers of water. But many parents also cannot afford the expensive lessons. School swimming is a good solution in this regard.

125571.explanation: To reduce drug nuisance caused by dealing in public, a legal location is much better. Then it can be regulated and enforced.

125575.explanation: A community center is the connecting factor in a neighborhood. It counteracts loneliness, and young people can find a place to hang out. A community center has many benefits.

125576.explanation: The center must remain accessible to everyone. Many people will avoid the center if prices rise. After the introduction of paid parking, far fewer people visited the center. Now that the first two hours are free, visits have increased again.

125577.explanation: The center should be made as car-free as possible. We should rather invest in better bicycle parking and good bus connections.

125579.explanation: If the environment changes due to construction projects, residents must be able to participate in the decision-making.

125580.explanation: It promotes health and is better for the environment.

statements.title: The municipality should involve residents more in new construction projects.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: In Veldhoven the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Veldhoven moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
In Veldhoven there should be more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every village and neighborhood in the municipality should have its own village center or community center.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Veldhoven should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should continue to give refugees with residence permits priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Paid parking in the city center should become more expensive.
Veldhoven should renovate/renew Library Veldhoven.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Residents of Veldhoven should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Veldhoven moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should do more to encourage walking and cycling.
